title: A Random Walk Down Wall Street
subtitle: The Time-Tested Strategy for Successful Investing
author: Burton G. Malkiel
themes: Stock prices follow a random walk and cannot be consistently predicted
Low-cost index funds outperform most actively managed funds over time
Diversification is the only free lunch in investing
Market timing and stock picking are futile for most investors
Behavioral biases systematically sabotage investor returns
Time in the market beats timing the market
Costs matter — fees compound into enormous wealth differences
awards: Over 1.5 million copies sold across 13 editions
Named one of the 100 Best Business Books of All Time
excerpt: A blindfolded monkey throwing darts at a newspaper's financial pages could select a portfolio that would do just as well as one carefully selected by experts. A Random Walk Down Wall Street is the definitive case for passive, index-based investing — backed by five decades of market data.
